name: browsing-memory-golden
description: Curated browsing-memory fixtures for retrieval and grounded-answer regression checks.

documents.title: React RSC Cache Notes
documents.content: React Server Components let the server render tree segments before the browser hydrates interactive islands. The notes explain that revalidatePath invalidates cached data for a route after a mutation. They also say server actions are a natural place to call revalidatePath when a form changes persistent data.

documents.title: Postgres JSONB Indexing Guide
documents.content: PostgreSQL JSONB fields work well with GIN indexes when queries need containment or key lookup. The guide says the containment operator can stay fast because GIN indexes map document terms to row locations. It warns that wide JSON documents still need careful index choice and query discipline.

documents.title: Docker Healthcheck Compose Notes
documents.content: Docker Compose healthchecks make startup order more reliable than sleeping for a fixed number of seconds. The article says depends_on with service_healthy waits for a container to become healthy before dependent services start. It recommends using a real HTTP or command probe instead of a blind timeout.

documents.title: OAuth Device Flow Notes
documents.content: The OAuth device flow gives the user a short code and asks them to verify on a second device or browser. The notes say the client should respect the polling interval from the authorization server and avoid hammering the token endpoint. They also stress keeping the verification URL and user code obvious in the UI.

documents.title: Prompt Injection Browser Agent Notes
documents.content: Browser agents should treat page content as untrusted because prompt injection can hide inside instructions, comments, or copied snippets. The notes recommend domain allowlists, explicit tool gating, and visible provenance for every action. They also say user confirmation should guard sensitive actions like external requests or file writes.

documents.title: Playwright Flaky Test Fixes
documents.content: Playwright tests get less flaky when they rely on locator auto-waiting instead of arbitrary sleeps. The article recommends expect polling, semantic locators, and a small number of focused end-to-end flows. It explicitly says fixed timeouts should be a last resort because they hide race conditions instead of fixing them.

documents.title: Retrieval Ranking and Reranking Notes
documents.content: A strong answer engine often starts with lexical retrieval like BM25, then adds semantic retrieval for recall, and finally reranks with a stronger model. The notes argue that hybrid retrieval gives better coverage than either lexical or vector search alone. They also say reranking improves precision by pushing the most relevant evidence to the top before synthesis.
